title: .NET Software Engineer
description: **Senior Software Engineer – €600/ Per Day** * Daily rate up to €600 per day * Full ownership of projects * Modern Tech Stack Step into a high impact role as a Senior .Net Engineer with a global US investment management company. Join at an exciting time as they grow their Dublin office from 17 to 50 people over the next 2 years. As a Senior .NET Developer you will have ownership and autonomy for the projects you are delivering on. The work you do will have a major impact on the whole business. You will be working on high\-impact transformative projects that will change how this global organisation with over $50BN in assets functions. Your work will have a meaningful impact on global processes. This global business is at the start of the AI adoption journey, and you will play a key role in this. The team is a small, high\-caliber unit of .NET Developers with extensive experience and expertise in C\# and .NET. The team is composed of high\-performing individuals who collaborate closely and work very well together. This is an exciting opportunity for an ambitious Senior .NET Engineer wanting to join a growing and successful company at the beginning of an AI adoption transformation **Why You’ll Love It Here** * **Daily rate** up to €600 * **High Technical Bar –** You will collaborate with developers working with modern technologies, at a high\-level and standard. * **High\-impact work** – Contribute to the overall growth of the organization * **Modern tech direction** – Large focus on software design and development to provide complex and scalable applications * **Strong team culture** – Collaborate with strong and experienced development team * **Flexibility** that actually works – just 1\-3 days onsite, the rest is remote * **Long\-term opportunity** – 6/12\-month contract with strong extension potential **What You’ll Do** * Develop innovative functionality in existing and new products across all business * Full involvement in the entire software development lifecycle to build automate solutions * Collaborate effectively with technical and non\- technical stakeholders stakeholders * Produce technical design documents and conduct technical walkthroughs **What you’ll bring** * 7\+ years of .NET skills * 7\+years of SQL development * Python/AI experience

title: 1st, 2nd or 3rd Year Hairdressing Apprentice
company: Agnes Burke Hair and Beauty Ltd
description: We are currently looking for a passionate and motivated **Hairdressing Apprentice** to join our growing salon team in Ireland. This is an exciting opportunity for a 1st, 2nd, or 3rd year apprentice who is eager to learn, grow, and develop their skills within a professional and supportive salon environment. About the Role: As an apprentice, you will work alongside experienced stylists while gaining hands\-on experience in all aspects of hairdressing. You will be supported throughout your training and encouraged to build confidence, creativity, and technical ability. Responsibilities: * Assisting senior stylists and salon team * Shampooing and preparing clients * Maintaining salon cleanliness and presentation * Greeting and looking after clients * Learning colouring, cutting, styling, and blow\-drying techniques * Supporting day\-to\-day salon operations * Attending training and continuing your apprenticeship education The Ideal Candidate: * Passionate about hair, beauty, and client care * Friendly, professional, and eager to learn * Strong work ethic and positive attitude * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * Reliable and motivated to grow within the industry What We Offer: * A supportive and welcoming team environment * Ongoing training and education * Hands\-on experience with experienced professionals * Opportunity to grow your skills and career * Exposure to modern colouring, cutting, styling, and client services * Flexible working hours Job Type: Full Time Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Employee discount Work Location: In person

title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €75k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: AI Support Engineer - Dublin
description: **About The Team** OpenAI’s User Operations team shepherds our customers’ adoption of AI and ensures that our customers' product experience is nothing short of exceptional. We are building the very first post\-AGI support team. We resolve complex issues, provide technical guidance, and support customers in maximizing value and adoption from deploying our products. We work closely with Sales, Technical Success, Product, Engineering and others, to deliver the best possible experience to our customers at scale. OpenAI's customers represent a range of diverse backgrounds and maturity, from early\-stage startups to established global enterprises. **About The Role** We are looking for dedicated, experienced, and passionate individuals to help solve some of the most difficult problems faced by our customers and build our post\-AGI support team with us. In this role, you will interact directly with customers through support tickets and Slack messages, troubleshooting complex issues and resolving novel, often undefined technical problems while setting a positive precedent for the rest of the team. You’ll partner closely with cross\-functional teams to drive initiatives that reduce bugs, improve features, and build the systems that elevate our customer experience. Your work will bring us toward industry\-leading response times and service levels, while strengthening our internal customer feedback operations in an increasingly intricate space. You will help scale our support organization by improving operational processes and leveraging our own technology to build the next version of the support team in the new AI world. You will be crucial to the success of the most innovative, disruptive, and high\-scale AI solutions being built with OpenAI. If you thrive in environments that value impact, collaboration, and fast\-paced problem\-solving, you might be the perfect fit for our team We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. **You’ll Be Responsible For** * Work directly with customers, solving their most complex problems and providing ownership and education on the use of our platforms. * Be among the foremost experts on everything related to OpenAI products, even where our AI does not have the answer. * Be among the last line of defense before our core Product and Engineering teams, and partner with engineering and customer teams to resolve issues. * Use scripting and emerging AI capabilities to improve internal tooling and automate recurring processes. * Take learnings from resolving customer issues and create our approach to scaling these solutions, partnering with product and Go\-To\-Market teams. * Orchestrate agentic improvements to our operations that will level\-up our entire team. * Foster a supportive and productive work culture within the User Operations team. * Provide support coverage in on call shifts and during holidays and weekends based on business needs. * Note: each person in this role will be expected to work a standard 5x8 work week, with 1 of the days covering weekend shifts each week (e.g., work weeks covering Tuesday \- Saturday or Sunday \- Thursday) **You Might Thrive In This Role If You Have** * Have 8\+ years of experience in user operations, technical support, or support engineering roles, ideally within tech startups or fast\-paced environments. * Are comfortable using emerging technologies (ie. Codex, ChatGPT, OpenAI API, etc.) to script or engineer code (e.g., Python or similar) for automating repetitive tasks and integrating tools. * Have expert\-level SaaS troubleshooting skills with the ability to rapidly understand new technologies and complex concepts. * Naturally question established norms, skillfully identify root causes, and proactively drive innovation and process improvements. * Are among the very best critical thinkers, problem solvers, and communicators of complex technical issues in the industry. * Thrive in ambiguity, adapt rapidly to change, continuously learn, and proactively seek opportunities for growth. * Have proven experience building strong relationships with customers and cross\-functionally to drive resolution to complex issues. * Have a humble attitude, an eagerness to help others, and a desire to pick up whatever knowledge you're missing to make both your team and our customers succeed. * Have high horsepower, are adept at frequent context switching and working on multiple projects at once with expansive ownership and prioritization. * Preferred: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, another relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience. **Compensation, Benefits And Perks** This is a position with OpenAI Ireland Ltd., which controls the hiring and management of this position. Total compensation includes an annual salary, generous equity, and benefits. * Medical, dental, and vision insurance for you and your family * Mental health and wellness support * PRSA plan with 8% employer matching * Unlimited time off * Annual learning \& development stipend ($1,500 USD equivalent per year) **About OpenAI** OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general\-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement. Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US\-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non\-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations. To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non\-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link. OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

title: Accelerated Growth Consultant, AGT, Google Customer Solutions (Romanian, English)
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience in advertising, consultative sales, business development, online media environment, or marketing role. * Ability to communicate in Romanian and English fluently to support client relationship management in this region. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience in hunting, acquisition, and within a start\-up environment. * Experience in planning, delivering and executing marketing campaigns, measurement and creative strategy. * Ability to own a portfolio in an advertising or media sales context, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. * Ability to move fast, be bold, and thrive in a dynamic, fast changing environment. * Ability to think strategically about complex issues and develop recommendations and action plans, with strong analytical skills. **About The Job** Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI\-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. **Responsibilities** * Deliver against ambitious quarterly revenue \& product growth targets, while prioritizing and delivering an outstanding experience to Google's advertisers within EMEA. * Build and manage a pipeline through proactive identification of high\-potential business in addition to close partnership with our marketing teams. * Establish relationships via phone calls or e\-mail marketing and design effective marketing strategies using expert product knowledge and deliver outstanding customer sales experience to drive long\-term success for our customers. * Consult with C\-level clients to achieve national and international online marketing and business goals through utilizing the full suite of Google’s marketing solutions (e.g., YouTube, Google Search and Display Network platforms). Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Administration & Support Coordinator 2
description: **WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHING** At AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next\-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. **Together, we advance your career.** **Administrative Support** * Provide day\-to\-day assistance to the Site Administration Manager. * Support the Wireless Team Administrator and provide ad hoc support to additional teams as needed. * Prepare visa letters, documentation, and general administrative outputs. * File documents, schedule meetings, and maintain organised records. **Visitor \& On\-Site Coordination** * Liaise with on\-site functions to host external visitors (agendas, visitor badges, logistics). * Manage room bookings for meetings and events via Outlook. * Create and track Facilities requests using Archibus. * Coordinate boardroom setup, including name tags, materials, and swag. **Event \& Meeting Support** * Assist in creating and coordinating PowerPoint presentations for Town Halls, VIP visits, and other events. * Provide hands\-on support at on\-site events (registration desk, microphone handling during All Hands, etc.). * Arrange catering, facilities services, and IT support for events and meetings. **Procurement \& Liaison** * Prepare and process purchasing requisitions for supplies. * Liaise with administrators and Executive Assistants across other AMD locations to ensure smooth cross\-site coordination. *Benefits offered are described:* AMD benefits at a glance. *AMD does not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee\-based recruitment services. title: Analytics Program Assoc
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** The **Analytics Development Program** is a full\-time opportunity designed for early career professionals eager to launch their careers in data analysis and data science. This program empowers participants to contribute within UnitedHealth Group, helping shape the future of healthcare through data\-driven insights. We're developing UnitedHealth Group's next generation of analytical leaders and seeking high\-performing high\-potential recent graduates to drive the future of healthcare. Participants will be supported by experienced program leads and managers who provide guidance and mentorship, helping them translate academic experience into real\-world value and efficiency across a large healthcare system. This role will require you be in office in a hybrid capacity and reside within a commutable distance of the following office: Dublin, Ireland **Please note:** Relocation assistance will not be available for this position. **The full\-time program will provide:** * Cohort based start date \- Tuesday, July 28, 2026 * 1 year program with final placement in July 2027 * Mentorship \& buddy system * Leadership exposure * Access to Live, self\-led training and development tools sessions * After completion of the Analytics Development Program, you will transition to a final placement role within the organization. This may be in the same team or an opportunity across the wider enterprise **Our program has a primary focus on the following:** * **Data Analysis** \- Using data visualization and statistics to find and share actionable insights * **Data Science** \- Building machine learning models to solve current healthcare challenges Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **Primary Responsibilities:** * Aid in the development, and implementation of new processes, applications, and enhancements to existing applications * Utilize pertinent data and facts to identify and solve a range of problems * Conceive and share insights to appropriate audiences * Subject matter expertise (SME) in the continued design and development of in\-house software applications * Collaborate with fellow data scientists and subject matter experts to design and oversee the development of working prototypes of data science solutions * Extract data for various reports and analyses, including database improvement and maintenance * Assess processes for efficiency and performance; utilize findings to successfully influence change * Design and implement new database tools * Support and drive analytic efforts around machine learning and innovation * Communicate analytic results and predictive models to business partners * Manipulation and analysis of data in support of a variety of analytic solutions * Identify and critique potential solutions to system/business challenges * Contribute to various research projects pertaining to business needs * Partner with cross\-functional teams to support strategic business initiatives * Other projects as assigned **Please note:** Your responsibilities will vary based on team and role. You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications:** * Final year Bachelor's degree candidate or Bachelor's degree obtained within the past 24 months from an accredited college or university; all degree coursework must be completed prior to the employment start date and verified within 6\-8 weeks following graduation, or equivalent experience * Completion of an analytics related course (e.g., data analysis, statistics, economics, machine learning * Proven experience working with SQL, data visualization, Python or Tableau * Proven eligibility to work in Ireland without company sponsorship **Preferred Qualifications:** * Currently pursuing a degree in Information Technology, Healthcare, Project Management, Business, Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Data Analytics, or another business related field * Demonstrated intermediate proficiency with PC based software applications and automated database management systems (e.g., Excel, Access, PowerPoint) * Proven previous experience in an analytical based internship * Proven relevant healthcare business related experience * Proven data analysis experience through student clubs, personal projects or other forms of employment **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA

title: Application Analyst
description: **18 month fixed term contract** (until September 2027\) An opportunity has arisen for an experienced ICT Applications Analyst to support the delivery, enhancement, and ongoing management of enterprise application solutions within a complex operational environment. The successful candidate will act as the lead Applications Analyst across key business and technology initiatives, working closely with stakeholders to identify opportunities for process improvement and technology enablement. Key Responsibilities * Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand operational requirements and identify opportunities to improve efficiency through technology and process enhancement. * Gather, analyse, and document user and system requirements for application solutions. * Evaluate business needs and design appropriate application solutions aligned with organisational architecture and technology standards. * Coordinate procurement activities in accordance with relevant governance and compliance requirements, including engagement with third\-party vendors and service providers. * Ensure effective integration of new and existing systems across the wider technology landscape. * Lead and coordinate user acceptance testing (UAT) activities to ensure solution quality and business readiness. * Manage the implementation of application solutions, including project planning, stakeholder communication, budgeting, scheduling, and vendor management. * Oversee ongoing support, maintenance, and enhancement of application systems while ensuring adherence to ICT standards, policies, and best practices. * Contribute to business continuity, risk management, and operational planning activities. * Support organisational obligations relating to health, safety, quality, and customer service standards. * Undertake additional responsibilities as required to support business and ICT objectives. Experience \& Skills Required * Minimum of two years’ experience delivering business systems implementation projects, with demonstrated involvement across the full project lifecycle from initiation through to implementation and ongoing support. * Experience in one or more of the following areas: * Business Analysis * Application Development * IT Project Management * Application Testing * Strong communication, stakeholder engagement, and interpersonal skills. * Working knowledge of Microsoft SQL Server. * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including experience with advanced functionality and macros. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and organisational skills.

title: Application Support Analyst
description: **Overview** As a Trading Application Support Analyst, you will join the post trade support team, providing technical and administrative support for all trading, operational, and support systems within the team. You will collaborate with development teams, Operations, and other key business stakeholders. This demanding and technical role requires strong technical systems and application knowledge to make critical support decisions under pressure. title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
description: **Python or Node.js \| Dublin Hybrid (1 day onsite) \| Contract** You’ll be joining a well\-established global business in Dublin to work on a real applied AI problem. This isn’t “throw an LLM at a PDF and hope for the best”. The team has a working proof of concept, and now needs a strong engineer to help turn it into something robust, scalable, and useful. You’ll be building systems that can ingest complex business documents, extract the important information, structure it properly, and support internal teams in making faster, better decisions. What you’ll be doing **You’ll work on:** * Document ingestion and processing pipelines * Extracting structured data from messy documents * Using LLMs where they genuinely add value * Building backend services, APIs, and workflows * Designing human\-in\-the\-loop systems * Helping shape the data model / ontology behind the product * Turning an early proof of concept into a proper production system **What you’ll need** You’ll need to be a strong engineer with either **Python or Node.js** . Useful experience includes: * Backend engineering * Document processing, PDF parsing, OCR, or extraction * LLMs in real\-world products * RAG, embeddings, semantic search, or document understanding * Working with unstructured or semi\-structured data * Designing clean data models around messy domains * Building systems in ambiguous, fast\-moving environments Some React exposure would be useful, but this is mainly backend / applied AI. **What this is not** This is not a pure ML role (they won't want ML engineers) You won’t be training models all day or doing academic research. This is applied AI in the real world: messy documents, unclear edges, humans in the loop, and systems that actually need to work. **The type of person who’ll do well** You’ll suit this if you like figuring things out, asking good questions, and turning vague problems into working software. You should be pragmatic, technically strong, comfortable with ambiguity, and happy working closely with domain experts. If you need every ticket perfectly written before you can move, this probably isn’t the one. If you like owning complex problems and building useful systems, it could be a great contract. In short A strong Python or Node.js engineer is needed to help build applied AI systems around complex document understanding. Real problem. Real users. Real business impact. No toy demos. No buzzword bingo. Just a chunky engineering problem that needs someone sharp. If you feel you're a fit, hit the apply button or ping me directly for a chat

title: Assistant Designer - Menswear
description: **Assistant Designer** Because your ideas are our style. Design, our way. As Assistant Designer, you will work closely alongside Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams to translate trend into product. Working alongside a Designer, you will collaborate with fellow product designers across the business you will create strong, fashion forward, commercial and global ranges that identify with the Primark brand. As Assistant Designer, you will demonstrate passion, curiosity and interest in fashion. You will predict key trends using the knowledge of top sellers, extensive research and the knowledge of forecasting tools. You will actively participate in key range building meetings and support product development through the completion of focused technical tasks. You will work within the development process, applying good knowledge of design tools, product qualities and specifications. You will be responsible for staying ahead of trends, continually research new ideas. **What You’ll Do As An Assistant Designer** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Research \& Spot Trends: Identify emerging fashion trends through research and translate them into commercially viable concepts for Primark’s ranges. * Support Range Development: Assist the Designer in building impactful, trend\-led product ranges that balance creativity and commerciality. * Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Designers, Buyers, and Merchandisers to ensure creative vision is executed and aligned with brand strategy. * Manage Design Process: Track design updates, maintain critical path timelines, and prepare trend and design files for presentations and supplier meetings. * Analyse Performance: Review past range performance with Buying and Merchandising teams to inform future design decisions. * Ensure Commercial Awareness: Understand cost implications, competitor activity, and how trends influence sales to support profitable product development. **Key Interactions and Engagements as part of this role:** As Assistant Designer you will report directly to a Designer. You will work closely with Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams, as well as collaborating with Design teams across all product categories to ensure alignment and a unified Design vision across our brand. You will also be expected to represent your product categories on working groups and forums across departmentally **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle. Let’s talk lifestyle: 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * Design Expertise: At least 2 years’ experience in a Trainee or Assistant Designer role, with a solid understanding of fashion design principles. * Relevant Qualification: Degree in fashion design or equivalent practical experience. * Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop to create high\-quality design assets. * Commercial Mindset: Good business acumen and genuine interest in retail, with the ability to balance creativity and commerciality. * Attention to Detail \& Organization: Accuracy, proactive approach, and excellent planning skills to manage deadlines effectively. * Collaborative \& Creative Attitude: Strong communication skills, positive energy, and an innovative mindset to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. Excited? Good. Because it’s energising to put your skills to work, in a growing global business committed to encouraging people to express themselves – and feel a sense of belonging. Apply to be part of Primark’s future, today… and enjoy career growth, our way. **About Primark** Primark is an international clothing retailer employing more than 70,000 colleagues across 16 countries in Europe and the US. Founded in Ireland in 1969 under the Penneys brand, Primark aims to create maximum joy at minimum cost – less money and less impact on the planet. Primark continues to expand across new and existing markets with the aim of reaching 530 stores by the end of 2026\. Every person impacts the culture at Primark. Our values: Caring (we always strive to put people first), Dynamic (we bravely push the boundaries to stay ahead) and Together (we learn more, laugh more and achieve more as a team) outline the behaviours we expect of our people as we work with each other, our customers, our suppliers and our partners. Let’s see what impact you can make! **REQ ID:** JR\-3870

title: Associate QC
description: **Overview:** Responsible for planning, executing and documenting the qualification, calibration and routine maintenance of laboratory systems for cGMP Quality Control laboratory facilities. Responsible for ensuring that all maintenance \& calibration activities carried out by Calibration Technicians are tracked \& managed through the Computerised Maintenance Management System (CMMS) Maximo. Candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation experience. **Key Responsibilities include:** \* Planning and conducting routine calibration, re qualification and maintenance of laboratory equipment ensuring calibration and maintenance schedules are adhered to as per CMMS Maximo. Reviewing \& filing of QC calibration \& maintenance documentation. \* Planning, executing and documenting the qualification of new laboratory equipment in a cGMP regulated environment, including the writing of equipment validation protocols and associated summary reports. \* Conduct periodic reviews of instrument validation as part of validation life cycle. \* Alerting Quality Control Management in the event of equipment failing to meet calibration or qualification requirements and conducting impact assessments/investigations as required. \* Scheduling and coordination of equipment repairs and maintenance with vendors/contractors, while minimizing level of down\-time for lab equipment, and disruption to laboratory activities. \* Participation in technical project teams to act as a subject matter expert on instrument validation regulations and procedures. \* Where applicable, owns and project manages change controls and adherence to Change Control metrics \* Supporting and managing the addition of project components to CMMA Maximo \* Act as a key contributor and sponsor to Data Integrity Assessments for Lab systems \* Any other tasks/projects assigned as per manger's request **Knowledge and Experience:** \* Bachelor's degree in a Science related field is required. \* Minimum of 3 years of laboratory experience in a cGMP laboratory environment including exposure to analytical instrument validation and data integrity assessments. \* Detailed knowledge of the methodology, and instrumentation and analytical techniques used for biopharmaceutical testing. \* Understanding of current regulatory requirements for cGMP laboratory equipment validation and electronic record integrity. \* Demonstrated success in managing an equipment qualification or maintenance program advantageous. The successful candidates will be involved with routine activities and may be requested to support project work. For the most part project work consists of the introduction and qualification of analytical equipment for use in routine QC analysis and the candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation

title: Automation / Efficiency Specialist
description: **SEI Investments** is a leading global provider of asset management and investment technology solutions. The company’s innovative solutions help corporations, financial institutions, financial advisors, and affluent families create and manage wealth. SEI is a people focused organization which encourages and recognizes employee development as being a critical component in the provision of excellent client service. An open, collaborative and detail oriented environment underpins this business objective. **Position Description** At SEI, **Automation Specialists** sit within the Alternative Funds business and play a crucial role in enhancing operations, blending financial operations knowledge with technical automation skills. The goal is to leverage a technical problem\-solving skillset to streamline complex fund processes for efficiency. The ideal candidate has deep familiarity with automation tools (notably Alteryx, Power Automate and the broader Power Platform, among others), solid programming/scripting abilities, and proven experience in the funds operations domain. The role entails a wide array of responsibilities – from process analysis and solution development to training and continuous improvement – acting as a bridge between the business and technology. This is an elite team operating in a rewarding, dynamic environment. **What You’ll Do** * Identify manual processes for automation: Working with business stakeholders (fund accountants, operations analysts, etc.) to pinpoint processes that are currently manual or inefficient and suitable for automation. * Design and develop automated solutions: Using appropriate tools to build automation workflows, design end\-to\-end solutions – such as data pipelines, report generation scripts, workflows – tailored to the needs of fund operations. * Testing and implementation: Rigorously testing automation workflows to ensure they function correctly and produce accurate results before deploying them into the business process. Implement the automated system into the team’s operations with minimal disruption, often rolling out in phases or parallel runs to validate outcomes. * Optimize and maintaining processes: Once automations are live, continuously monitor performance and outcomes. Troubleshoot issues or errors that arise and make adjustments to optimize speed and reliability. Update automations as underlying systems or requirements change (for example, adapting a workflow when a new data source becomes available). * Documentation and compliance: Documenting all automated processes and workflows. This includes keeping an audit trail of what the automation does, parameters used, and results. * Training \& support: Train or educate team members on how to use the new tools or workflows. Act as a support contact for troubleshooting if end\-users encounter issues with the automated systems. **What You Will Bring To The Table** * Expertise in data automation and workflow tools is essential. Strong skills in Alteryx for data prep and analytics, proficiency with RPA/workflow software like UiPath or Power Automate, and familiarity with databases (SQL) and programming (e.g. Python, VBA) * Financial domain knowledge \- understanding of Alternative Funds operations and financial processes (e.g. fund accounting, capital calls, investor reporting) is highly valued, enabling tailored automation solutions to industry\-specific needs * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities are a must to analyze complex processes and design efficient solutions * Communication skills are critical for collaborating with stakeholders and training teams * Project management skills to assist in juggling multiple automation projects and meeting deadlines * Proficiency in using data visualization and business intelligence tools like Tableau, Power BI, or QlikView/Sense is an advantage. To enable integration of solutions with reporting dashboards or produce data outputs that feed into BI tools. * General IT skills and integration knowledge \- knowledge of how to integrate with APIs, understanding system interfaces, and familiarity with cloud platforms or software development lifecycle is also an advantage. A well\-rounded technical capability. * Appetite to continuously learn and remain technically versatile * A strong understanding of good data management practices. This includes data governance, data quality, and ensuring that automated solutions align with organizational data standards. * Applicants are expected to bring a Product Manager mindset to the role. Some automation solutions developed may serve as prototypes or foundational components for larger, enterprise\-wide implementations. The ability to think strategically and scale solutions is key. **Attributes We Value** * Ability to multi\-task, prioritise work and manage time to maximize efficiency and to meet strict deadlines. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously * Ability to ask relevant and targeted questions to get to the core of the task * Ability to think logically and sequentially * Strong oral, written and interpersonal communication skills. * Fluency in English Language (both verbal and written) essential * Individual who takes initiative and ownership of assigned responsibilities * Positive ‘can do’ attitude with excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail * Someone who will embody our SEI Values of courage, integrity, collaboration, inclusion, connection and fun **Benefits You Can Expect** Healthcare for yourself, your spouse and any dependents under 18 years old. Pension Scheme, Life Assurance \& Group Income Protection. Education Assistance, Sports Club Supplement, Tax Saver Travel Benefits, 2 Paid Volunteer Days per year, Employee Share Participation Scheme and Competitive Family \& Annual Leave Entitlements. We are focused on ensuring a healthy work\-life balance and offer hybrid working model and flexible working hours. **SEI is an Equal Opportunity Employer and so much more…** We recognize that our people are our most valuable asset and are (literally) invested in your success; we know that a healthy, happy and motivated workforce is key to our continued growth. We are focused on ensuring a healthy work\-life balance and offer our employees benefits which include private medical care for you and your family, educational assistance scheme and actively encourage work life balance. **AI Acceptable Use In The Application And Interview Process** SEI acknowledges the growing integration of artificial intelligence (AI) tools into individuals’ personal and professional lives. If you intend to incorporate the use of any AI tools at any stage of the application and/or interview process, please ensure you have reviewed and adhere to our AI use guidelines.

There will be opportunities for training and regular clinical supervision is provided. This temporary post is assigned to the Coolock Children’s Disability Network Team. Staff will see children in the Coolock CDNT offices in Coolock Primary Care Centre, Cromcastle Road. Children will also be seen at home and in preschool/school. The successful candidate must have the following: Essential criteria: Degree or Masters in Social Work or equivalent National Qualification in Social Work (NQSW) Be fully registered with CORU Be committed to the delivery of high quality, person\-centred, community\-based services Have excellent communication, time management skills and the ability to work in a self directed manner. Full clean drivers license and access to a car Desirable criteria: Experience working in children’s disability services. Have experience working as part of a multidisciplinary team. A panel will be created for future employment opportunities, across the four St Michael’s House Children’s Disability Network Teams. Pending graduates may apply. Benefits of working in St Michael's House HSE Pay Scale (incremental\*) Premium Payments (Frontline staff) Generous Annual Leave Sick Pay Scheme Paid Maternity Leave Pension Career Progression Cycle to Work Scheme Employee Assistance Programme Training / CPD To Apply A comprehensive CV, detailing education, skills, career history, experience. A short cover letter/personal statement (i.e., no more than 2 pages) outlining why you wish to be considered for the post and where you believe your skills, experience and values meet the requirements of this role with St. Michael’s House. Only candidates shortlisted for interview will be contacted. Salary scale : €50,621 \- €73,080 LSI Should you have no prior public sector experience you will be placed on point 1 of the pay\-scale above Informal enquires: Niamh Giltinan: Coolock Children’s Disability Network Manager, niamh.giltinan@smh.ie Catherine Rafter: Principal Social Worker, Catherine.rafter@smh.ie Closing Date: 26th May 2026, 5:00pm We endeavour to give as much notice as possible for the interview dates etc. Candidates should make themselves available on the date(s) All interviews will be held in person Candidates should note that canvassing will disqualify them.
